title: Gradient Descent Algorithm
description: Basic gradient descent for finding local minimum of multivariable function
explanation: Starts from initial guess and iteratively moves opposite to gradient direction. Alpha is learning rate. Converges to local minimum at (0,0) for this convex function.

title: Newton-Raphson for Multivariable
explanation: Uses second-order Taylor approximation. Converges quadratically near optimum. For f=x²+y², converges in one iteration from any point.

complexity.time: O(n³) for matrix inversion in each iteration

title: Lagrange Multiplier Solver
description: Implementation to find extremum subject to constraint
explanation: Solves system of equations from Lagrange multiplier method. Creates three equations from gradient conditions and one constraint equation.

complexity.time: Depends on solving method - typically O(1) for small systems

title: Hessian Matrix Computation and Classification
description: Code to compute Hessian and classify critical point
code:
// Compute Hessian and classify critical point
function classifyCriticalPoint(f, x0, y0):
 // Compute second partial derivatives
 fxx = partial(f, x, x) // Second derivative w.r.t x
 fyy = partial(f, y, y) // Second derivative w.r.t y
 fxy = partial(f, x, y) // Mixed partial
 
 // Evaluate at critical point
 Hxx = fxx(x0, y0)
 Hyy = fyy(x0, y0)
 Hxy = fxy(x0, y0)
 
 // Compute determinant of Hessian
 D = Hxx * Hyy - Hxy * Hxy
 
 // Classification
 IF D > 0 THEN:
 IF Hxx > 0 THEN:
 RETURN "Local Minimum"
 ELSE:
 RETURN "Local Maximum"
 ELSE IF D < 0 THEN:
 RETURN "Saddle Point"
 ELSE:
 RETURN "Test Inconclusive"
explanation: Uses second derivative test. Computes Hessian matrix and its determinant to classify critical points. Works when Hessian is non-singular.

complexity.time: O(1) - constant time for fixed number of partials
